The bottom-right 2x2 corner is the key distinguishing feature—only the target has all four cells filled there. Looking at other patterns, the target's top row has two consecutive X's followed by a gap and another X, while the distractors show different arrangements. The last column heavily favors the target with four X's compared to three in Distractor 1 and just two in Distractor 2. The first column also shows variation across the options.

The final row clinches it: the target displays "X □ □ X X" with two empty cells in the middle, whereas both distractors use alternating patterns. This combination of the filled bottom-right corner and the specific arrangement in the last row makes the target uniquely identifiable.
